subject: First Aid & Safety
title: The Role of Asymptomatic Carriers
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Role of Asymptomatic Carriers usable.

hook.text: 'Typhoid Mary' infected 51 people and never felt sick a day in her life. She was an asymptomatic carrier.

buildup.text: Asymptomatic carriers harbor and shed pathogens without showing any symptoms of illness.

discovery.text: Up to 40-45% of COVID-19 infections were transmitted by people who never developed symptoms.

twist.text: You can't quarantine what you can't see—asymptomatic spread makes containment nearly impossible.

climax.text: Population-wide measures like masking work because they protect against invisible transmission.

punchline.text: No symptoms doesn't mean no spread. The most dangerous carriers feel fine.

quiz.question: What made 'Typhoid Mary' so dangerous?
options: She spread typhoid while showing no symptoms herself
